advanced/anonymous-lambdas.title: Anonymous Lambda Resolution
advanced/anonymous-lambdas.description: Compose uses lambdas extensively. Scaffold, NavHost, Column, Row, LazyColumn -- all take @Composable lambda parameters. Each lambda is a composable function that Rebound instruments. Without name resolution, the composable tree is 80% ``.

advanced/architecture.title: Architecture
advanced/architecture.description: Rebound consists of five modules that span compile time, runtime, and developer tooling.

advanced/custom-budgets.title: Custom Budgets
advanced/custom-budgets.description: The compiler plugin classifies composables automatically based on name patterns and IR structure. When the heuristic gets it wrong, use the @ReboundBudget annotation to override.

advanced/kmp-support.title: KMP Support
advanced/kmp-support.description: The Rebound runtime is a Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP) library. The compiler plugin instruments composable functions regardless of target platform.

advanced/kotlin-versions.title: Kotlin Version Support
advanced/kotlin-versions.description: Rebound supports Kotlin 2.0.x through 2.2.x and beyond. The Gradle plugin automatically selects the correct compiler artifact based on your project's Kotlin version.

cli/commands.title: Commands
cli/commands.description: All commands are run via the rebound-cli.sh script from the repository root.

cli/setup.title: Setup
cli/setup.description: The Rebound CLI is a Bash script (rebound-cli.sh) that communicates with the app's runtime over an ADB-forwarded socket. No additional dependencies beyond adb, nc (netcat), and python3 are required.

concepts/budget-classes.title: Budget Classes
concepts/budget-classes.description: Budget classes are the core concept in Rebound. Each @Composable function is assigned a budget class that defines its maximum acceptable recomposition rate. The budget depends on what the composable does, not on a single flat threshold.

concepts/changed-mask.title: $changed Bitmask
concepts/changed-mask.description: The Compose compiler injects $changed parameters into every @Composable function. Rebound decodes these bitmasks at runtime to tell you exactly which parameters caused each recomposition.

concepts/dynamic-scaling.title: Dynamic Scaling
concepts/dynamic-scaling.description: Rebound dynamically adjusts budget thresholds based on the current interaction state. A composable that would violate its budget during idle may be perfectly acceptable during a fast scroll or animation.

concepts/how-it-works.title: How It Works
concepts/how-it-works.description: Rebound operates in three phases: compile-time instrumentation, runtime tracking, and data transport to consumers.

concepts/skip-tracking.title: Skip Tracking
concepts/skip-tracking.description: Skip rate measures how often the Compose runtime skips a composable's body. It is one of the most revealing metrics for understanding composition efficiency.

concepts/violations.title: Violations
concepts/violations.description: A violation occurs when a composable's recomposition rate exceeds its budget, adjusted for the current interaction state.

getting-started/first-run.title: First Run
getting-started/first-run.description: After adding the Rebound Gradle plugin, here is how to see it in action.

guides/ci-integration.title: CI Integration
guides/ci-integration.description: CI budget gates are on the Rebound roadmap. This page describes the planned approach and how it will work. The implementation is not yet available.

guides/debugging-recompositions.title: Debugging Recompositions
guides/debugging-recompositions.description: This guide walks through a complete debugging workflow using Rebound, from noticing a problem to shipping a fix.

guides/fixing-violations.title: Fixing Violations
guides/fixing-violations.description: This guide covers common violation patterns organized by budget class, with before/after code examples for each.

ide-plugin/editor-integration.title: Editor Integration
ide-plugin/editor-integration.description: Rebound integrates directly into the Android Studio editor so you can see recomposition data without switching to the tool window. Research on developer tooling consistently shows that context-switching between profiler windows and source code is where time goes to die.

ide-plugin/history-tab.title: History Tab
ide-plugin/history-tab.description: The History tab enables session persistence, VCS-correlated comparison, and regression detection across code changes.

ide-plugin/hotspots-tab.title: Hot Spots Tab
ide-plugin/hotspots-tab.description: The Hot Spots tab provides a flat, sortable table of every instrumented composable, ranked by severity. It answers the question: "Which composables need attention right now?"

ide-plugin/installation.title: Installation
ide-plugin/installation.description: The Rebound IDE plugin adds a tool window to Android Studio that provides live recomposition monitoring, hot spots analysis, timeline visualization, and editor integration.

ide-plugin/monitor-tab.title: Monitor Tab
ide-plugin/monitor-tab.description: The Monitor tab is the primary view in the Rebound tool window. It shows a live composable hierarchy with real-time metrics.

ide-plugin/settings.title: Settings
ide-plugin/settings.description: The Rebound IDE plugin settings are accessible at Settings > Tools > Rebound in Android Studio. Settings are persisted via PersistentStateComponent in the project's .idea/rebound.xml file.

ide-plugin/stability-tab.title: Stability Tab
ide-plugin/stability-tab.description: The Stability tab surfaces parameter-level stability data from the $changed bitmask, helping you identify which specific parameters are causing recompositions.

ide-plugin/timeline-tab.title: Timeline Tab
ide-plugin/timeline-tab.description: The Timeline tab displays a composable-by-time heatmap, showing recomposition patterns over time. It reveals temporal patterns that point-in-time snapshots cannot: sustained hotspots, burst patterns, and correlation between composables.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: Rebound is a Compose recomposition budget monitor. It assigns each @Composable function a recomposition rate budget based on what that composable does, and alerts you when the budget is exceeded.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.description: Rebound is under active development. This page tracks planned features and their current status.
